title: Delicate Slovakian Cheese Quiche
description: A tangy and light Slovakian dish featuring a cheese pull, plain chocolate, and an en-croute crust, friendly to Buddhist temple dietary preferences. This quiche is perfect for a snack or dessert.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the pie crust and place it in a tart pan with a removable bottom.
In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, sour cream, salt, and black pepper.
Add the shredded Slovakian cheese to the bowl and stir until well combined.
Melt the unsalted butter and pour it into the bowl, stirring until the mixture is smooth.
Add the chopped plain chocolate to the bowl and stir until well combined.
Pour the cheese and chocolate mixture into the pie crust.
Bake the quiche for 35-40 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the center is set.
Remove the quiche from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes.
Serve the quiche warm, sliced into wedges.
